sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐν Ἄργει γʼ οὐδʼ ἐπʼ Εὐρώτα ῥοαῖς .
sections.translation: No ; he is neither in Argos nor by the streams of the Eurotas .

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: αἰαῖ · κακὸν τόδʼ εἶπας οἷς κακὸν λέγεις .
sections.translation: Alas ! This is evil news for those to whom you bring it .

sections.label: Section 126
sections.original: ὡς κεῖνος ἀφανὴς σὺν δάμαρτι κλῄζεται .
sections.translation: He is said to have disappeared with his wife .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.original: οὐ πᾶσι πορθμὸς αὑτὸς Ἀργείοισιν ἦν ;
sections.translation: Wasn ’ t there the same passage for all the Argives ?

sections.label: Section 128
sections.original: ἦν , ἀλλὰ χειμὼν ἄλλοσʼ ἄλλον ὥρισεν .
sections.translation: Yes ; but a tempest scattered them in every direction .

sections.label: Section 129
sections.original: ποίοισιν ἐν νώτοισι ποντίας ἁλός ;
sections.translation: On which surface of the salty ocean ?

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: μέσον περῶσι πέλαγος Αἰγαίου πόρου .
sections.translation: While they were crossing the Aegean in mid-channel .

sections.label: Section 131
sections.original: κἀκ τοῦδε Μενέλαν οὔτις εἶδʼ ἀφιγμένον ;
sections.translation: And from that time does no one know of Menelaos ’ arrival ?

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: οὐδείς · θανὼν δὲ κλῄζεται καθʼ Ἑλλάδα .
sections.translation: No one ; but throughout Hellas he is reported to be dead .

sections.label: Section 133
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα · Θεστιὰς δʼ ἔστιν κόρη ;
sections.translation: I am wholly lost . Is the daughter of Thestius alive ?

sections.label: Section 134
sections.original: Λήδαν ἔλεξας ; οἴχεται θανοῦσα δή .
sections.translation: You speak of Leda ? She is dead and gone , indeed .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: οὔ πού νιν Ἑλένης αἰσχρὸν ὤλεσεν κλέος ;
sections.translation: It wasn ’ t Helen ’ s disgraceful fame that killed her , surely ?

sections.label: Section 136
sections.original: φασίν , βρόχῳ γʼ ἅψασαν εὐγενῆ δέρην .
sections.translation: Yes , they say she tied a noose around her noble neck .

sections.label: Section 137
sections.original: οἱ Τυνδάρειοι δʼ εἰσὶν ἢ οὐκ εἰσὶν κόροι ;
sections.translation: Are the sons of Tyndareus still alive or not ?

sections.label: Section 138
sections.original: τεθνᾶσι καὶ οὐ τεθνᾶσι · δύο δʼ ἐστὸν λόγω .
sections.translation: They are dead , and not dead : it is a double story .

sections.label: Section 139
sections.original: πότερος ὁ κρείσσων ; ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγὼ κακῶν .
sections.translation: Which report is the stronger ? I am so unhappy at these evils !

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: ἄστροις σφʼ ὁμοιωθέντε φάσʼ εἶναι θεώ .
sections.translation: Men say that they are gods in the likeness of stars .

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας τοῦτο · θάτερον δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: That is good news ; but what is the other story ?

sections.label: Section 142
sections.original: σφαγαῖς ἀδελφῆς οὕνεκʼ ἐκπνεῦσαι βίον .
sections.translation: That they killed themselves because of their sister . But enough of such talk ! I do not need to grieve twice . As to why I came to this royal palace ,

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: τὴν θεσπιῳδὸν Θεονόην χρῄζων ἰδεῖν ,
sections.translation: wanting to see the prophetess Theonoe , you be my patron , so I might obtain an oracle : how I should steer a favorable course to the island of Cyprus , where Apollo has declared I am to live , giving it the island name of

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: Σαλαμῖνα θέμενον τῆς ἐκεῖ χάριν πάτρας .
sections.translation: Salamis in honor of that fatherland over there .

sections.label: Section 151
sections.original: πλοῦς , ὦ ξένʼ , αὐτὸς σημανεῖ · σὺ δʼ ἐκλιπὼν
sections.translation: The voyage itself will explain that , stranger ; leave this land and escape , before the son of Proteus , the ruler of this land , catches sight of you ; now he is away with his trusty hounds tracking his savage quarry to the death ;

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: κτείνει γὰρ Ἕλληνʼ ὅντινʼ ἂν λάβῃ ξένον .
sections.translation: for he kills every visitor from Hellas that he catches . Do not seek to learn his reason , and I will not say ; for how could I help you ?

sections.label: Section 158
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας , ὦ γύναι · θεοὶ δέ σοι
sections.translation: Lady , you have spoken well . May the gods grant you a return for your kindness !

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: Ἑλένῃ δʼ ὅμοιον σῶμʼ ἔχουσʼ οὐ τὰς φρένας
sections.translation: Although you have a body like Helen ’ s , your heart is not like hers , but very different . May she die miserably , and never reach the streams of Eurotas ! But may you always have good fortune , lady .

sections.label: Section 164
sections.original: ὤ , μεγάλων ἀχέων καταβαλλομένα μέγαν οἶκτον
sections.translation: Oh , as I begin the great lament of my great distress ,

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: ποῖον ἁμιλλαθῶ γόον ; ἢ τίνα μοῦσαν ἐπέλθω
sections.translation: what mourning shall I strive to utter ? or what Muse shall I approach with tears or songs of death or woe ? Alas !

sections.label: Section 1.167
sections.original: πτεροφόροι νεάνιδες ,
sections.translation: Winged maidens , virgin daughters of Earth , the Sirens , may you come to my mourning

sections.label: Section 1.170
sections.original: μόλοιτʼ ἔχουσαι Λίβυν
sections.translation: with Libyan flute or pipe or lyre , tears to match my plaintive woes ; grief for grief and mournful chant for chant , may Persephone send choirs of death

sections.label: Section 1.175
sections.original: Φερσέφασσα
sections.translation: in harmony with my lamentation , so that she may receive as thanks from me , in addition to my tears , a paean for the departed dead beneath her gloomy roof .

sections.label: Section 1.179
sections.original: κυανοειδὲς ἀμφʼ ὕδωρ
sections.translation: Beside the deep-blue water

sections.label: Section 1.180
sections.original: ἔτυχον ἕλικά τʼ ἀνὰ χλόαν
sections.translation: and on the tangled grass , I happened to be drying purple robes in the sun ’ s golden blaze near the young reed shoots ; from my mistress , from where she cried aloud her misery ,

sections.label: Section 1.185
sections.original: ὅμαδον ἔκλυον , ἄλυρον ἔλεγον ,
sections.translation: I heard a sound , a mournful song not fit for the lyre , because she was then shrieking , lamenting with her wails ; just as a Naiad nymph , who sends a song of woe ringing over the hills , cries out , under the rocky hollows , with screams

sections.label: Section 1.190
sections.original: Πανὸς ἀναβοᾷ γάμους .
sections.translation: at the rape of Pan .

sections.label: Section 2.191
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ ·
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Maidens of Hellas , the prey of barbarian sailors ! An Achaean sailor

sections.label: Section 2.195
sections.original: τις ἔμολεν ἔμολε δάκρυα δάκρυσί μοι φέρων .
sections.translation: came , he came bringing tears upon tears to me . Ilion has been destroyed and is left to the enemy ’ s fire through me , the death-giver , through my name , full of suffering .

sections.label: Section 2.200
sections.original: Λήδα δʼ ἐν ἀγχόναις
sections.translation: Leda sought death by hanging , in anguish over my disgrace . My husband , after much wandering in the sea , has died and is gone ;

sections.label: Section 2.205
sections.original: κάστορός τε συγγόνου τε
sections.translation: and Castor and his brother , twin glory of their native land , have vanished , vanished , leaving the plains that shook to their galloping horses ,

sections.label: Section 2.210
sections.original: δονακόεντος Εὐρώ-
sections.translation: and the schools of reed-fringed Eurotas , scene of youthful labors .

sections.label: Section 2.211
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! for your mournful fate and destiny , lady ! You were fated , fated to have a life full of pain , when Zeus begot you on your mother ,

sections.label: Section 2.215
sections.original: χιονόχρως κύκνου πτερῷ
sections.translation: shining through the air on the wings of a snow-white swan . What evil is not yours ? What life have you not endured ? Your mother is dead ;

sections.label: Section 2.220
sections.original: δίδυμά τε Διὸς οὐκ εὐ-
sections.translation: the twin beloved sons of Zeus do not enjoy happiness ; and you do not see your fatherland , while through the cities a rumor goes , mistress , which hands you over

sections.label: Section 2.225
sections.original: πότνια , παραδίδωσι λέχεσιν ,
sections.translation: to the bed of a barbarian ; your husband has lost his life in the salty waves , and never again will you bring glee to your father ’ s halls and Athene of the Bronze House .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , τίς ἢ Φρυγῶν
sections.translation: Ah ! Who was it , either from Phrygia

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: ἢ τίς Ἑλλανίας ἀπὸ χθονὸς
sections.translation: or from Hellas , who cut the pine that brought tears to Ilion ? From this wood the son of Priam built his deadly ship , and sailed by barbarian oars

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: τὰν ἐμὰν ἐφʼ ἑστίαν ,
sections.translation: to my home , to that most ill-fated beauty , to win me as his wife ; and with him sailed deceitful and murderous Kypris , bearing death for the Danaans .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινα συμφορᾶς .
sections.translation: Oh , unhappy in my misfortune ! But Hera , the holy beloved of Zeus on her golden throne , sent the swift-footed son of Maia . I was gathering fresh rose leaves in the folds of my robe ,

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἔσω πέπλων ῥόδεα πέταλα ,
sections.translation: so that I might go to the goddess of the Bronze House ; he carried me off through the air to this luckless land , and made me an object of miserable strife , of strife between Hellas and the sons of Priam . And my name

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: παρὰ Σιμουντίοις ῥοαῖσι
sections.translation: beside the streams of Simois bears a false rumor .

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: ἔχεις μὲν ἀλγείνʼ , οἶδα · σύμφορον δέ τοι
sections.translation: You have sorrows , I know ; but it is best to bear as lightly as we can the necessary evils of life .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: φίλαι γυναῖκες , τίνι πότμῳ συνεζύγην ;
sections.translation: Dear friends , to what a fate am I yoked ? Did my mother bear me as a wonder to mankind ? For no other woman , Hellene or barbarian , gives birth to a white vessel of chicks , in which they say Leda bore me to Zeus .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: τέρας γὰρ ὁ βίος καὶ τὰ πράγματʼ ἐστί μου ,
sections.translation: My life and all I do is a wonder , partly because of Hera , and partly my beauty is to blame . If only I could be rubbed out like a painting , and have again in turn a plainer form instead of beauty , and the Hellenes would have forgotten the evil fate that I now have ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: Ἕλληνες ἐπελάθοντο , τὰς δὲ μὴ κακὰς
sections.translation: and would remember what part of my life is not evil , as they now remember what is .

sections.label: Section 267
sections.original: ὅστις μὲν οὖν ἐς μίαν ἀποβλέπων τύχην
sections.translation: When someone looks to one event only , and is ill-treated by the gods , it is hard , but all the same it can be borne . But I am involved in countless troubles .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν οὐκ οὖσʼ ἄδικος , εἰμὶ δυσκλεής ·
sections.translation: First , although I never acted wrongly , my good name is gone . And this trouble is stronger than the reality , if someone incurs blame for wrongs that are not his own . Next , the gods have removed me from my native country to barbarian habits , and bereft of friends

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: δούλη καθέστηκʼ οὖσʼ ἐλευθέρων ἄπο ·
sections.translation: I have become a slave although I am free by birth ; for among barbarians all are slaves except one . And the only anchor of my fortunes is gone , the hope that my husband would come one day and free me of my woes — he is dead , he no longer exists .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: μήτηρ δʼ ὄλωλε , καὶ φονεὺς αὐτῆς ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: My mother is dead , and I am called her murderer — unjustly , but that injustice is mine to bear ; while the one who was born the glory of the house , my daughter , is growing gray as a virgin , without a husband ; and those two Dioskouroi , called the sons of Zeus , are no more .

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: οὐκ ἐστόν . ἀλλὰ πάντʼ ἔχουσα δυστυχῆ
sections.translation: But with all my misfortunes , I am as good as dead in my circumstances , though not in fact . And this is the last evil of all : if ever I should come home , I would be shut out by barred doors , for people would think I was that Helen of Troy , coming back with Menelaos .

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: εἰ μὲν γὰρ ἔζη πόσις , ἀνεγνώσθημεν ἂν
sections.translation: If my husband were still alive , we could have recognized each other by recourse to tokens which are evident to us alone . But now this is not so , and he can never be saved .

sections.label: Section 293
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἔτι ζῶ ; τίνʼ ὑπολείπομαι τύχην ;
sections.translation: Why then do I still live ? What fortune do I have left ? Shall I choose marriage in exchange for evils ,
